source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED §5

primaryText: "I know the answer," whispered the student to the teacher.
translation: "I know the answer," whispered the student to the teacher.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_whisper_transitive. Quote + PP goal: the PP does not compete for Case licensing, so QI remains grammatical (Storment 2026, section 5).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED §3

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_whisper_pronoun. Pronominal subjects degrade QI — full DPs are preferred postverbally (Storment 2026, section 3).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED §5 eq. (125)

primaryText: "There is danger ahead," warned Mary John.
translation: "There is danger ahead," warned Mary John.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_warn_double_obj. QI blocked with two DP arguments — the transitivity constraint from Case licensing: T0 can license only one DP. 'Warn' is naturally ditransitive, so the failure isolates the transitivity constraint rather than confounding it with verb subcategorization.

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED §5

primaryText: Into the room kicked the ball the player.
translation: Into the room kicked the ball the player.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean li_kick. LI blocked with a transitive verb — both QI and LI show the transitivity constraint (Storment 2026, section 5).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED §3

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean li_arrive_pronoun. LI categorically blocks pronominal subjects (cf. QI, which merely degrades them, section 3).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (40)

primaryText: "Avoid beef," advise all the New Age dieticians.
translation: "Avoid beef," advise all the New Age dieticians.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_agreement_english. The verb 'advise' (plural) agrees with the postverbal agent 'dieticians' (plural), not with the quote (Storment 2026, section 3.1).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (77)-(80)

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_agreement_setswana. The subject marker surfaces as default class-17 'go' instead of first-singular 'ke' — default agreement; the agent is not in Spec,TP (Storment 2026, section 3.1).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (62a)

primaryText: "We should leave," thought Max without actually saying __.
translation: "We should leave," thought Max without actually saying __.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_parasitic_gap_blocked. Parasitic gap blocked in QI: the theme undergoes A-movement (smuggling), which cannot license parasitic gaps (Storment 2026, section 3.2).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (62b)

primaryText: "We should leave," Max thought without actually saying __.
translation: "We should leave," Max thought without actually saying __.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_parasitic_gap_baseline. Non-QI preposed quote licenses the parasitic gap: the quote undergoes A-bar movement (topicalization).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (67a)

primaryText: "Where did the wheat go?" seemed to say the heron.
translation: "Where did the wheat go?" seemed to say the heron.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_raising. The quotative theme raises from embedded to matrix Spec,TP — an A-movement chain (Storment 2026, section 3.3).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (87)

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_conjoint_disjoint. The disjoint morpheme 'a' is blocked in QI: the agent remains vP-internal, in the conjoint domain (Storment 2026, section 3.4).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (90)

primaryText: "But," said Frodo, "that is a long way off."
translation: "But," said Frodo, "that is a long way off."
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_quote_split. The quote can split around verb + agent: it sits in DiscourseP, not as a single constituent in Spec,TP (Storment 2026, section 3.5).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (96a)

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_quote_nongrammatical. 'Pop!' is not a grammatical sentence — the quote is clause-external (Storment 2026, section 3.5).

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (11)

comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_ordering_depictive. The depictive 'a tagilwe' (drunk) follows the agent — depictives are vP-external adjuncts and stay in situ when the VP smuggles.

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (21)

primaryText: 'Titi o molato,' go bua kgosi kapela.
translation: 'Titi is guilty,' says the chief quickly.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_ordering_manner. The manner adverb 'kapela' (quickly) follows the agent — manner adverbs are vP-external and stay in situ.

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (17)

primaryText: 'Molelo!' go go-ile Seabelo go kopa thuso.
translation: 'Fire!' went Seabelo to call for help.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_ordering_purpose. The purpose clause 'go kopa thuso' (to call for help) follows the agent — purpose clauses are vP-external adjuncts.

source.paperLabel: UNVERIFIED (27)

primaryText: 'Ke a go rata,' go batla go bua Seabelo.
translation: 'I love you,' wants to say Seabelo.
comment: Migrated from Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Unaccusativity/Data.lean qi_ordering_complement. The control complement 'batla go bua' (want to say) precedes the agent — VP-internal material moves with the VP via smuggling.
